# MentionDrop MCP Review

Last modified: |  Fred Magni

Tired of your AI agents operating in a data vacuum? MentionDrop MCP is the game-changer you’ve been waiting for. This innovative tool transforms your intelligent assistants into proactive market analysts by providing them with live market signals, directly from the public web. Acting as a dedicated MCP server, it empowers agents like Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, and Hermes to query brand mentions, competitor conversations, and customer pain points without the tedious manual scraping of dashboards.

MentionDrop MCP Review

Visit Website  Have an AI Tool? Submit it here!

## Empowering Your AI Agents

MentionDrop MCP acts as a dedicated market intelligence feed for your AI. Instead of asking a blank chat box to guess, agents gain permission to query specific, high-signal public sources like Reddit and Google News. This means your AI can: [...] ## The MCP Advantage

MentionDrop MCP isn’t just another integration; it’s a dedicated Machine Cognition Protocol (MCP) server. It provides a secure, account-scoped API endpoint, ensuring your agent only accesses relevant, permissioned data. This architecture is purpose-built for AI agents that need to turn raw signals into actionable intelligence, allowing you to move from reactive monitoring to proactive, agent-assisted market engagement.

| Traditional Monitoring | MentionDrop MCP with AI Agents |
 --- |
| Manual dashboard checks. | Agent automatically sifts, summarises, and prioritises. |
| Slow, human-centric response drafting. | Agent generates first-pass reply drafts instantly. |
| Fragmented data from multiple sources. | Unified, high-signal data feed via a single endpoint. |

results.title: MentionDrop: Stop Finding Brand Mentions Too Late
results.content:
### Summaries, not raw link dumps

Every surfaced mention includes sentiment and a suggested next step so you know what deserves attention.

### MCP for AI agents

Connect Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Hermes, or your own agent to digests, pain signals, and reply drafts.

Read MCP docs

### HTTP API included

Use account-scoped API keys to pull mentions, keywords, digests, and reply drafts into your own workflows.

View API docs

### 14-day usefulness guarantee

If MentionDrop is too noisy or does not feel useful, email us within 14 days and we will refund you.

Built for moments that expire

## Not another dashboard to babysit.

MentionDrop is for the mentions where being early changes the outcome: a comparison thread, a complaint, a launch mention, or someone asking for alternatives. [...] ### MCP for AI agents

Let Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Hermes, or your own agent query mentions, competitor signals, demand signals, and reply drafts.

### Noise filters

Only the mentions worth your time. Broad or ambiguous keywords are flagged before they create noise.

## Built for people who act fast

Google Alerts was made for casual curiosity. MentionDrop is built for people who need to respond before the thread moves on.

### Detection speed

MentionDropMinutes

Google AlertsOften delayed

By the time a slow alert arrives, the useful part of the conversation may already be over.

### Feature comparison

MinutesDetectionOften delayed

AI summariesSummariesRaw links only

Bounded sourcesCoverageGoogle index only

Reddit + selected webForumsLimited context [...] MentionDrop helps small teams monitor brand, product, and competitor mentions across bounded high-signal public sources without paying enterprise social listening prices. Instead of dumping raw links into your inbox, it summarizes what happened, highlights the tone, and suggests what to do next. Teams can also pull the same signals into scripts, internal tools, and AI agents through the HTTP API and MCP endpoint.

That makes it useful for launch monitoring, brand protection, customer research, and finding buying-intent conversations in places like forums, blogs, Reddit threads, and news sites. If you have ever searched for a reliable Google Alerts alternative, this is the workflow most teams actually want: timely alerts, useful filtering, and a clear next step.

## How MentionDrop works [...] MentionDrop logoMentionDrop

MCP docs

# MentionDrop MCP setup and tool reference

MentionDrop exposes a public MCP endpoint backed by the v1 API and your account API keys. Connect it to Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Hermes, or any MCP-aware client to query brand mentions, competitor signals, demand signals, digests, and reply drafts.

## Quick start

Create an API key in Settings, then verify it works:

```
curl  \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY"
```

A 200 response confirms your key is valid and shows your plan metadata. Then add the MCP endpoint to your agent client below.

## Endpoint and auth

```
 Authorization: Bearer md_live_xxx
``` [...] ```
 Authorization: Bearer md_live_xxx
```

MCP uses the same API-key auth, account scoping, plan gates, and service logic as the HTTP API. Keys are prefixed `md_live_`. Create one in Settings.

## Ask your agent to set it up

Paste this prompt into Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, or any MCP-aware client and it will find the right config file and add MentionDrop for you.

```
Add MentionDrop as an MCP server. Endpoint:  Auth header: Authorization: Bearer md_live_YOUR_KEY Find my MCP config file and add the server entry. If unsure which file, check for claude_desktop_config.json or .cursor/mcp.json.
```

Replace `md_live_YOUR_KEY` with your API key from Settings before pasting.

## Generic MCP client config
